Classification of Crossovers

Author

jmontero

Published

December 29, 2024

Classification model for predicting CO regions from random genomic regions. Three classifiers available (decision_tree/logistic_reg/rand_forest). Select the number of folds for cross-validation, number of iterations for bayesian optimization and criterium to choose the best parameters.

Setting up environment

Packages

commonly_used_packages = c("data.table", "dplyr", "data.table", "ggplot2", "tidyr", "stringr")
new_packages = c("tidymodels", # Essential for ML
                 "finetune", # For Bayes tuning
                 "doFuture", # For parallel processing
                 "tune", # Tuning support with tidymodels
                 "rpart", # Decision Tree engine
                 "glmnet", # Logistic regression with regularization support
                 "ranger", # Random Forest
                 "parsnip", # Boosted trees
                 "vip", # Feature importance with ranger
                 "knitr") # For table creation
pkgs = c(commonly_used_packages, new_packages)
for (pkg in pkgs) { if (!suppressMessages(require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE))) { install.packages(pkg, character.only = TRUE, repos = "https://cran.uni-muenster.de/", quiet = TRUE) ; require(pkg, character.only = TRUE, quietly = TRUE) } }

Arguments and Parameters

- Running Random Forest on the file haploRILs_10_2_3_edited 
- Bayes optimization with 300 iterations 
- PRECISION as criterium for best parameter selection 
- 10-fold cross-validation 
- IMPURITY-based importance computation 
- 40 cores are being used 
- The seed is 19381

Importing Data

# Import the feature table
if (execution_mode == "linux") {
  data = fread(paste0(prdir, filename, ".bed"))
  feature_info = fread(paste0(ftdir, "feature_info"))
} else {
  data = fread(paste0(filename, ".bed"))
  feature_info = fread(paste0("feature_info"))
}

Pre-modelling

Data Preprocessing

Adding homoeologous group as grouping factor

Apply required data classes. Targets must be factors and features numeric. Add homologous pair as grouping variable.

# Create a grouping variable for homoeologous chromosomes
data = data %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      str_detect(chr_id, "A01|C01") ~ "grp1",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A02|C02") ~ "grp2",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A03|C03") ~ "grp3",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A04|C04") ~ "grp4",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A05|C05") ~ "grp5",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A06|C06") ~ "grp6",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A07|C07") ~ "grp7",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A08|C08") ~ "grp8",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A09|C09") ~ "grp9",
      str_detect(chr_id, "A10") ~ "grp10",
      TRUE ~ "unknown" # Catch unexpected cases
    )
  )
Conversions of data class and format
# Convert to tibble for tidymodels compatibility
data = as_tibble(data)
# Extract the integer features
integer_columns = data %>% select(where(is.integer)) %>% colnames()
# Convert integer columns to double
data = data %>% dplyr::mutate(across(all_of(integer_columns), as.double))
# Convert target to factor
data = data %>% dplyr::mutate(Crossovers = factor(Crossovers, levels = c(1, 0)),
                              group = factor(group))

Recipe and Scaling

Scale features by subtracting the mean and dividing by standard deviation. The mean and stdev of the resulting distribution will be 0 and 1 respectively. Use the formula \(Crossovers .vs. Features\).

# Make recipe
recipe_obj = recipe(Crossovers ~ ., data = data) %>%
  update_role(c(starts_with("chr_"), group), new_role = "ID") %>% # Treat chr_id and group as IDs, not predictors
  step_zv(all_predictors()) %>% # Remove predictors with unique values
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) # Standard scaling of all predictors
#### DO NOT APPLY THE RECIPE TO THE DATA SET YET!!!
#### THAT WOULD CAUSE DATA LEAKAGE
#### DO IT DURING CROSS-VALIDATION

Cross-Validation Setups

# Set up cross-validation for tuning (e.g., k-fold cross-validation)
cv_folds = group_vfold_cv(data, v = k, group = group)
Applying 10-fold cross-validation on data. 
# Group 10-fold cross-validation 
# A tibble: 10 × 2
   splits              id        
   <list>              <chr>     
 1 <split [7512/762]>  Resample01
 2 <split [7474/800]>  Resample02
 3 <split [7480/794]>  Resample03
 4 <split [7626/648]>  Resample04
 5 <split [7584/690]>  Resample05
 6 <split [6586/1688]> Resample06
 7 <split [7824/450]>  Resample07
 8 <split [7296/978]>  Resample08
 9 <split [7444/830]>  Resample09
10 <split [7640/634]>  Resample10

  Assessment Groups:  grp2

  Assessment Groups:  grp6

  Assessment Groups:  grp1 
Fold 4:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp3, grp4, grp5, grp6, grp7, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp8 
Fold 5:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp3, grp4, grp5, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp9 
Fold 6:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp4, grp5, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp3 
Fold 7:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp3, grp4, grp5, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp9 
  Assessment Groups:  grp10 
Fold 8:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp3, grp4, grp5, grp6, grp8, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp7 
Fold 9:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp3, grp4, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp5 
Fold 10:
  Training Groups:  grp1, grp2, grp3, grp5, grp6, grp7, grp8, grp9, grp10 
  Assessment Groups:  grp4

Define Metrics

# ROCAUC, Accuracy, precision and recall
metric_set = metric_set(roc_auc, accuracy, precision, recall)
print(metric_set)
A metric set, consisting of:
- `roc_auc()`, a probability metric | direction: maximize
- `accuracy()`, a class metric      | direction: maximize
- `precision()`, a class metric     | direction: maximize
- `recall()`, a class metric        | direction: maximize

Modelling

Model Specifications

if (classifier == "decision_tree") {
  model = decision_tree(
    cost_complexity = tune(),
    tree_depth = tune(),
    min_n = tune()
  ) %>%
    set_engine("rpart") %>%
    set_mode("classification")
  param_grid = hardhat::extract_parameter_set_dials(model)
} else if (classifier == "logistic_reg") {
  model = logistic_reg(
    penalty = tune(),
    mixture = tune()
  ) %>%
    set_engine("glmnet") %>%
    set_mode("classification")
  param_grid = hardhat::extract_parameter_set_dials(model)
} else if (classifier == "rand_forest") {
  if (importance_method == "permutation") {
    model = rand_forest(
      mtry = tune(),
      min_n = tune(),
      trees = tune()
    ) %>%
      set_engine("ranger", importance = "permutation") %>%
      set_mode("classification")
  } else if (importance_method == "impurity") {
    model = rand_forest(
      mtry = tune(),
      min_n = tune(),
      trees = tune()
    ) %>%
      set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity") %>%
      set_mode("classification")
  }
  param_grid = hardhat::extract_parameter_set_dials(model)  %>%
    update(mtry = finalize(mtry(), data %>% select(-starts_with("chr"), -Crossovers, -group)))
} else if (classifier == "boosted_trees") {
  model = boost_tree(
    mtry = tune(),
    trees = tune(),
    learn_rate = tune(),
    tree_depth = tune(),
    loss_reduction = tune(),
    min_n = tune()
  ) %>%
    set_engine("xgboost") %>%
    set_mode("classification")
  param_grid = hardhat::extract_parameter_set_dials(model) %>%
    update(mtry = finalize(mtry(), data %>% select(-starts_with("chr"), Crossovers, -group)))
}
print(model)
Random Forest Model Specification (classification)

Main Arguments:
  mtry = tune()
  trees = tune()
  min_n = tune()

Engine-Specific Arguments:
  importance = impurity

Computational engine: ranger 
cat("Parameter grid: ", "\n")
Parameter grid:  
print(param_grid)
Collection of 3 parameters for tuning

 identifier  type    object
       mtry  mtry nparam[+]
      trees trees nparam[+]
      min_n min_n nparam[+]

Workflow Setup

# Create a workflow that combines the recipe and the model
workflow = workflow() %>%
  add_model(model) %>%
  add_recipe(recipe_obj)
print(workflow)
══ Workflow ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Preprocessor: Recipe
Model: rand_forest()

── Preprocessor ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2 Recipe Steps

• step_zv()
• step_normalize()

── Model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Random Forest Model Specification (classification)

Main Arguments:
  mtry = tune()
  trees = tune()
  min_n = tune()

Engine-Specific Arguments:
  importance = impurity

Computational engine: ranger

Hyperparameter Tuning

# Set up control for Bayesian tuning to optimize parallel processing
control_bayes_opt = control_bayes(
  verbose = TRUE,
  no_improve = 50,
  parallel_over = "everything",
  save_pred = TRUE
)
# Perform Bayesian optimization
#set.seed(123)
tune_results = tune_bayes(
  workflow,
  resamples = cv_folds,
  param_info = param_grid,
  iter = n_iter,
  initial = 50,
  metrics = metric_set,
  control = control_bayes_opt
)
❯  Generating a set of 50 initial parameter results

best_params = select_best(tune_results, metric = criterium)
These are the best parameters based on precision: 
# A tibble: 1 × 4
   mtry trees min_n .config
  <int> <int> <int> <chr>  
1     1  1909    38 Iter6  
# Finalize the workflow with the tuned parameters
final_workflow = finalize_workflow(workflow, best_params)
print(final_workflow)

Preprocessor: Recipe
Model: rand_forest()

── Preprocessor ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2 Recipe Steps

• step_zv()
• step_normalize()

── Model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Random Forest Model Specification (classification)

Main Arguments:
  mtry = 1
  trees = 1909
  min_n = 38

Engine-Specific Arguments:
  importance = impurity

Computational engine: ranger

Model Fitting

# Fit the model on the full data
final_fit = fit(final_workflow, data = data)
saveRDS(final_fit, paste0(outputname, "_", classifier, ".final_fit.rds"))

Feature Importance

Computing Importance Magnitude
# Assign title according to importance computation method
importance_title = if (importance_method == "coefficient") "Coefficient-based Importance" else if (importance_method == "impurity") "Impurity-based Importance" else if (importance_method == "permutation") "Permutation-based Importance"
# Calculate importance based on the classifier and feature importance computation
if (classifier == "logistic_reg" && importance_method == "coefficient") {
  importance_data = final_fit %>%
    extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
    tidy() %>%
    dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Importance = abs(estimate),
      Direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, "negative", "positive"),
      Importance_type = importance_title
    ) %>%
    dplyr::rename(Feature = term) %>%
    dplyr::select(Feature, Importance, Direction, Importance_type)
} else if (classifier %in% c("decision_tree", "rand_forest", "boosted_trees")) {
  if (classifier == "rand_forest" || importance_method == "impurity") {
    importance_data = final_fit %>%
      extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
      vip::vi()
  } else if (classifier != "rand_forest" && importance_method == "permutation") {
    importance_data = final_fit %>%
      extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
      vip::vi_permute(
        train = data[, -which(names(data) == "Crossovers")],
        target = data$Crossovers,
        metric = "roc_auc",
        smaller_is_better = FAlSE,
        nsim = 200,
        pred_wrapper = function(model, newdata) {
          predict(model, newdata, type = "prob")[[".pred_1"]]
        },
        event_level = "first"
      )
  }
}
Calculating Impurity-based Importance
